commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A repair worker can make the deadline-gate and retention-policy fixes mechanically, while maintainer policy approval remains separate after the branch is refreshed.; Needs attention: The diff has a concrete security-boundary issue: the autoban deadline gate can enforce account bans without a post-deadline confirming score.; Review finding: [P1] Require the confirmation score after the warning deadline — conve... commands.review_summary:
Adds warning-first publisher-abuse autoban enforcement, an admin kill switch, scheduler changes, warning/ban email handling, schema state, docs/spec updates, and regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR defect: source inspection shows a warned nomination bans when warningExpiresAt has passed and latestScoreId changed, without checking whether that score was produced after the deadline. A focused repro is a warned candidate whose latest score changes before the deadline, then the autoban drain runs after the deadline.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A repair worker can make the deadline-gate and retention-policy fixes mechanically, while maintainer policy approval remains separate after the branch is refreshed.

target.title: Warn before publisher abuse autobans
target.body:
## Summary
- Changes publisher-abuse automatic enforcement to warning-first: the first eligible `potential_ban_candidate` sends a warning email with a one-week deadline, and a later eligible scan can ban only if a newer score still lands in the ban tier after the deadline.
- Adds an admin-only kill switch on `/management?view=abuse` that stops scheduled publisher-abuse warning and banning.
- Runs publisher-abuse pressure and temporal scoring weekly, while keeping the one-account backup autoban drain for missed work.
- Excludes staff-linked and official publishers before scoring/enforcement, and sends missing-email/linkage cases to manual review.

## What Changed
- Adds persisted warning state to publisher-abuse nominations: sent time, deadline, score id, and run id.
- Adds `autoban_warning_sent` review events and a publisher-abuse warning email with concrete score signals and remediation steps.
- Keeps actual bans on the shared account-ban path, so token revocation, owned listing hiding, audit logs, normal suspension email, and appeal unban matching stay in the existing flow.
- Adds a `publisherAbuseAutobanEnabled` system setting with admin mutation, moderator-readable query, and audit logging.
- Moves the kill switch into the abuse management header and removes the unused System management placeholder.
- Updates operator docs and the moderation security spec for weekly warning-first enforcement.

## Verification
```text
bunx convex codegen
bun run test -- convex/publisherAbuse.test.ts convex/crons.test.ts convex/lib/emails.test.ts src/routes/-management.test.tsx
bun run ci:types-build
bun run ci:unit
bun run format:check
bun run lint
bun run check:peers
bun run deadcode:ci
bun run ci:packages
git diff --check
```

Results:
```text
focused tests: 4 files passed, 121 tests passed
ci:types-build: passed
ci:unit: passed, 264 files passed, 1 skipped, 3284 tests passed, 1 skipped
format:check: passed
lint: passed
check:peers: passed
deadcode:ci: passed
ci:packages: passed
git diff --check: passed
convex codegen: passed, no generated file changes
```

Blocked:
```text
bun run ci:static fails at bun audit before format/lint/deadcode. Current advisories:
- dompurify <=3.4.10 via mermaid/monaco-editor: GHSA-cmwh-pvxp-8882
- undici >=7.23.0 <7.28.0: GHSA-vmh5-mc38-953g, GHSA-pr7r-676h-xcf6
```
target.author: jesse-merhi
